name: Down-Home Mashed Potatoes
recipeIngredient: Milk as needed (quarter to half-cup per potato)
Salt and white pepper, to taste
recipeInstructions:
Mashed potatoes need not be perfectly smooth; in fact, a few small lumps improve the texture. Nor should the milk be premeasured and heated: The quantity needed will depend on the type and age of the potatoes. (Mealy baking potatoes that have been stored for a long time take more milk than new potatoes.) Gradually pour milk into the potatoes, beating after each addition until they are just right. A few minutes in the oven before serving will reheat and puff up the potatoes.
1. Bring a large pot of salted water to boil. Meanwhile, peel potatoes and cut them into quarters or halves (depending on size). Add potatoes to the pot as you peel them, even if the water is not yet boiling. Cook until fork-tender, about 25 minutes after the water returns to a boil. Some pieces can be left slightly underdone. Drain well.
2. Transfer potatoes and butter to a large bowl. Using a potato masher or fork, coarsely mash together. Make a well in the center and pour in a small amount of milk.
3. With an electric mixer (avoid using a blender or food processor, which will make the potatoes too starchy), beat the potatoes, adding more milk gradually as needed until the desired texture is achieved. Taste for salt and pepper. Potatoes can be set aside until nearly time to serve.
4. Preheat oven to 350°F about one hour before serving.
5. Spoon mashed potatoes into an oven-safe casserole dish, sprinkle with paprika, and bake uncovered for 20 minutes.
